diagrams.title: TCP Header Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the 20-byte TCP header with all fields used for flow control and reliability
elements: Data Offset (4 bits) + Reserved (3 bits) + Flags (9 bits)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding which fields implement flow control (Window Size) and reliability (Sequence, ACK, Flags)

diagrams.title: TCP Sliding Window Operation
diagrams.description: Visual representation of sliding window moving as ACKs are received
elements: Usable Window region (can be sent immediately)
Sequence numbers 1-10 shown with window covering 4-7
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how window slides forward upon receiving ACKs, enabling pipelined transmission

diagrams.title: Three-Way Handshake Process
diagrams.description: Step-by-step connection establishment between client and server
elements: Server sends SYN-ACK with seq=y, ack=x+1
Client sends ACK with seq=x+1, ack=y+1
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how TCP initializes sequence numbers and prepares for data transfer

diagrams.title: Flow Control vs Congestion Control
diagrams.description: Comparison diagram showing different control mechanisms in TCP
elements: Flow Control: Sender → [Receiver Buffer] → Rate based on rwnd
Congestion Control: Sender → [Network Routers] → Rate based on cwnd
Different triggers and mechanisms for each
diagrams.purpose: Clarifying the difference between end-to-end flow control and network-level congestion control
